theorems.name: Radial limits of mock theta functions
theorems.reduces: replace divergent radial-limit numerics by a finite roots-of-unity formula
prerequisites: identify the mock theta function type
identify the shadow or modular completion
invariant_hints: minimal polynomial of the resulting algebraic value
verification_hints: compare with low-precision numerical radial approach only as a sanity check
preferred_recipe: work in an exact cyclotomic field
compute the algebraic value before minpoly
avoid_patterns: do not shrink epsilon repeatedly near a root of unity
do not truncate a divergent q-series at floating-point q
theorems.cas_hint: A future exact algebraic-number sidecar should evaluate the finite cyclotomic sum and compute minpoly.

theorems.name: Eta-quotient evaluation at rational points
theorems.reduces: turn eta-products at rational points into exact finite products or modular transformations
preferred_recipe: only then combine with other q-series values
avoid_patterns: do not approximate an infinite product near a root of unity
do not vary truncation lengths as a substitute for exact cusp analysis
theorems.cas_hint: Use exact cyclotomic arithmetic for finite products; Wolfram can simplify the resulting algebraic expressions when represented symbolically.

theorems.name: Construction of harmonic weak Maass forms and CM evaluations
theorems.reduces: this is a construction/research task: first choose and justify a theoretical construction, then use CAS only for targeted verification
invariant_hints: Hecke action on the holomorphic part
verification_hints: verify the principal part at cusps
verify rationality of traced coefficients for small Hecke indices
preferred_recipe: choose Poincare series, xi-preimage, Eisenstein derivative, or theta lift
use CAS for coefficient and CM checks only
avoid_patterns: do not blindly explore modular-form spaces with repeated CAS calls
do not try to discover the Maass form numerically
theorems.cas_hint: Theory-first. Use a CAS only after specifying the construction, e.g. to verify q-expansion coefficients or numerical CM evaluations.
